commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fa7423fafaf3f91b731d875dfe6253324dbdf7fe)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes iMessage text-send RPC timeout handling to recover persisted sent rows, conditionally use a one-shot `send-rich` fallback for owned clients, add regression tests, and document inbound `chat.db` troubleshooting.

PR surface: Source +89, Tests +118, Docs +25. Total +232 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection shows current main rethrows generic text RPC timeouts outside the approval-prompt recovery path, and the PR's mocked tests exercise that boundary. I did not reproduce the failure against a live macOS Messages account.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer needs to decide the long-lived client ownership/fallback strategy and require live proof; this is not a safe narrow automation repair.

target.title: fix #90850: iMessage send can wedge on long-lived RPC; add one-shot send-rich fallback and receive-side troubleshooting
target.body:
## Summary

- Add guarded recovery for iMessage RPC text sends that time out after dispatch.
- Recover already-persisted outbound rows before considering any fallback send.
- Limit one-shot `imsg send-rich` fallback to RPC clients owned by the current send call, after the owned client is stopped, and keep caller-owned long-lived clients from resending.
- Add receive-side troubleshooting for the case where inbound iMessages never reach local `chat.db`.

Fixes #90850

## Maintainer readiness

- **Fix classification:** Root-cause bug fix for iMessage message-delivery timeout recovery.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** 86/100. The code path, fallback gates, docs, lint, typecheck, and focused tests are covered locally; the remaining gap is live macOS Messages delivery, which is not available from this Linux environment.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** When the long-lived iMessage RPC sender wedges, agent replies can be generated but never reach Messages. Users then see a configured and apparently healthy iMessage channel while outbound replies silently fail or cannot be correlated back to the sent GUID.
- **Scope boundary:** This only changes iMessage text-send timeout recovery and the iMessage troubleshooting docs. It does not change media sends, channel routing, gateway lifecycle, account configuration, receive implementation, or non-iMessage channels.
- **What did NOT change:** Media sends, channel routing, gateway lifecycle, account configuration, receive implementation, non-iMessage channels, and successful RPC send behavior are not changed; this PR only narrows the timeout recovery boundary for text sends.
- **Source-of-truth boundary:** `chat.db` remains the source of truth for proving an outbound send already landed. The patch does not introduce a new message schema, config key, provider protocol, or persistence format.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Public contract surface is unchanged: no schema, config, protocol, migration, or default setting changes. The canonical confirmation source remains `chat.db`; the related open PR scan found no same-contract or canonical PR; Apple Push and macOS Messages sync repair are out of scope.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** Existing successful RPC sends keep the same path. Timeout fallback requires a sent-row check first; one-shot `send-rich` is only attempted for a client created by the current call after that owned client is stopped. Caller-owned RPC clients keep the previous no-resend behavior on ambiguous timeouts.
- **Related open PR scan:** daily-fix linked-PR and public-contract scans found no related open PR for this issue or the detected `send.rich` / `chat.db` contract terms.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This does not attempt to repair Apple Push, macOS Messages sync, SIP/private bridge launch state, or stale inbound delivery; the docs add troubleshooting steps for those receive-side conditions.
- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback helper returns `null` only for unsupported/unavailable CLI fallback errors, preserving the original RPC timeout otherwise. Timeout regression tests mock `Date.now()` so the chat.db polling path is deterministic. `expect.any(Number)` is used only for the dynamic `sentAfterMs` timestamp passed into the sent-row resolver.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A timed-out iMessage RPC text send can now recover the original persisted GUID, and only uses one-shot `send-rich` when the current call owns and has stopped the RPC client first. Caller-owned RPC clients and approval prompts do not resend after ambiguous timeouts.
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A long-lived iMessage RPC `send` timeout no longer leaves generic text sends with only a hard failure when the original sent row can be recovered, and it avoids duplicate sends when the original in-flight RPC cannot be safely owned or stopped.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout exercising the iMessage extension send path with the repository's Vitest extension harness, plus docs, lint, typecheck, format, and whitespace checks. No live macOS Messages account or real `chat.db` was available in this environment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts --reporter=verbose
corepack pnpm format:check -- extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts docs/channels/imessage.md
corepack pnpm docs:check-mdx
corepack pnpm lint:extensions -- extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > recovers generic text sends already written by a timed-out rpc send
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > falls back to one-shot send-rich when rpc text send times out before writing chat.db
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > throws the rpc timeout when one-shot send-rich fallback is unavailable
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > does not fallback to send-rich for caller-owned rpc clients
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > throws the rpc timeout without resending when sent-row checks are unavailable

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  35 passed (35)

All matched files use the correct format.
Docs MDX check passed (677 files, 11630ms).
corepack pnpm lint:extensions -- extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
git diff --check
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The focused iMessage send tests pass with coverage for recovered persisted sends, self-owned `send-rich` fallback, unavailable fallback, caller-owned no-resend behavior, and missing sent-row check no-resend behavior. Formatting, MDX docs validation, extension lint, extension typecheck, and whitespace checks all pass.
- **What was not tested:** Live outbound or inbound iMessage delivery on macOS was not tested because this Linux environment cannot run the Messages bridge, access a real `~/Library/Messages/chat.db`, or receive Apple Push/iMessage traffic.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** RPC `send` timeout for generic text now covers persisted-row recovery, self-owned `send-rich` fallback, unavailable fallback, caller-owned no-resend behavior, unavailable sent-row checks, and approval prompt no-resend behavior.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** `sendMessageIMessage` is the boundary that decides whether a timed-out RPC send is recovered, retried through one-shot CLI, or surfaced as the original timeout, so these tests lock the duplicate-send safety rules at the decision point.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root cause is an invariant gap in the iMessage text-send timeout path: generic text RPC timeouts were treated as unrecoverable, while the only recovery logic was scoped to approval prompts. The code did not first consult the `chat.db` source-of-truth boundary to prove whether the original send had already landed before deciding between recovery, fallback, or preserving the timeout.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The invariant is that an ambiguous timeout must not resend unless the original in-flight sender is under this call's ownership and has been stopped. The new flow checks for an already-persisted sent row first, returns that GUID without resending when found, and only invokes `send-rich` after stopping an owned RPC client; caller-owned clients keep the original timeout behavior to avoid duplicates.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk:message-delivery`.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes iMessage outbound timeout behavior and adds a fallback path, so the main risk is duplicate outbound messages after an ambiguous RPC timeout.
- **Why acceptable:** The fallback is intentionally gated: sent-row recovery runs first, approval prompts do not fallback, caller-owned RPC clients do not fallback, and self-owned clients are stopped before one-shot `send-rich` is attempted. Regression tests cover the safe recovery path and the no-resend boundaries.
